title: aulomyrcia citrifolia
intro: Aulomyrcia citrifolia, also known as the Tamarillo or Tree Tomato, is a type of flowering plant native to South America. The plant's leaves, fruits, and roots have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Aulomyrcia citrifolia include reducing oxidative stress, improving heart health, and providing antioxidant properties, among others. The plant is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed fresh or dried to harness its medicinal benefits.
